description_long: A vibrant cluster of wildflowers with slender green stems and a mix of pastel-colored blooms, including shades of lavender, pink, and yellow. The flowers have numerous thin, radiating petals that create a soft, feathery appearance. The stems are long and upright, supporting the blooms above a dense base of foliage. The leaves are lanceolate and light green, adding to the natural, fresh look. The arrangement is loose and organic, resembling a wildflower patch commonly found in urban parks or roadside gardens. The overall impression is one of delicate beauty and natural diversity, with each flower head slightly differing in color and size.
description: A lush cluster of pastel wildflowers with thin, radiating petals and long green stems, featuring pink, lavender, and yellow blooms. The arrangement is airy and natural, with lanceolate leaves and a dense base.
description_view_0: Front view shows a dense cluster of pastel flowers with the most blooms visible, highlighting the variety of colors and the upright stems.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the depth of the cluster, with some stems and blooms extending further out, and more green foliage visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the flower heads and more stems, with fewer blooms facing forward and more green leaves apparent.
description_view_3: Angled view shows a mix of flower faces and profiles, emphasizing the layered arrangement and the spread of the stems.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, forming daisy-like flower heads with multiple layers of thin, elongated petals radiating symmetrically from the center. There is clear overlapping and layering, giving a full, feathery appearance.
